description_long: This traffic barricade is a metal structure with a yellow finish, showing signs of weathering and rust, indicative of prolonged outdoor use. It consists of a horizontal top rail and a matching bottom rail, connected by evenly spaced vertical bars, forming a sturdy rectangular grid. The ends of the barricade curve smoothly into the ground supports, which are flat, perpendicular feet designed to provide stability on flat surfaces like roads or sidewalks. The overall construction is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, focusing on durability and visibility. The yellow paint is chipped and worn, revealing the underlying metal in places, and there are no visible reflective stripes or warning signage. The barricade is lightweight enough to be moved by one or two people but heavy enough to resist being easily knocked over.
description: A weathered yellow metal traffic barricade with vertical bars and flat, stable feet, designed for urban crowd or traffic control.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the barricade, with all vertical bars and both curved end supports visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the barricade and the flat, perpendicular support foot.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the vertical bars and end supports from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the perpendicular support foot.
